questions.question: Explain the working principle of Linear Search algorithm.
Compare Linear Search and Binary Search in terms of time complexity, space complexity, and data requirements.
keyPoints: Linear: O(n) time, O(1) space, works on unsorted data
Binary: O(log n) time, O(1) space, requires sorted data
Binary better for large sorted data

questions.question: Write a C function to implement Linear Search on an array of integers and explain your code.
keyPoints: Function signature with array, size, target parameters

questions.question: Analyze the time complexity of Linear Search for best case, average case, and worst case scenarios with proper justification.
keyPoints: Best case: O(1) - element at first position
Average case: O(n) - element in middle, n/2 comparisons
Worst case: O(n) - element at end or not present

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of Linear Search and provide examples of real-world applications where it would be appropriate to use.
keyPoints: Advantages: simplicity, works on unsorted data, no extra memory
Disadvantages: inefficient for large datasets, sequential access only
Applications: small datasets, unsorted data, linked lists
When to choose over other algorithms
